selected_validations.reason: The generator must produce a repo-ready client website launch pack, not just a loose prose prompt.
required_evidence: generated codex-build.prompt.md
generated website build mission
generated client intake summary
generated content-needed checklist
generated technical brief
generated validation plan
generated deployment checklist

selected_validations.reason: Outputs must cover assessment evidence, client needs, content gaps, SEO/AEO strategy, technical constraints, and validation strategy.
required_evidence: every major recommendation traces to assessment evidence, client intake, or documented assumption
missing or stale assessment data is called out
missing client details are listed instead of invented

selected_validations.reason: Client website prompts must produce local service websites focused on services, service areas, discoverability, and conversion.
required_evidence: homepage requirements include services, service area, trust proof, and CTA
core service page requirements are present
priority service-area page requirements are present
FAQ/AEO requirements are present
LocalBusiness or service schema guidance is present when applicable
sitemap and crawlability requirements are present

selected_validations.reason: Generated prompts must avoid repeating known assessment/report failure modes.
required_evidence: does not claim missing service-area pages without evidence or verification
does not claim broken links or images without URLs or verification
does not create thin doorway service-area pages
does not invent reviews, licenses, credentials, service guarantees, locations, or project proof
does not bury contact actions below decorative content

selected_validations.reason: Generated prompts must enforce the current Northvalley client website technical baseline.
required_evidence: repo target is https://github.com/Northvalley-Intelligence
default branch is main
Next.js with TypeScript and React baseline is specified
npm and package-lock.json are specified
Node 22 CI baseline is specified
@opennextjs/cloudflare is specified
wrangler deployment is specified
open-next.config.ts and wrangler.jsonc are required
ESLint, typecheck, Vitest, Playwright, and npm audit checks are required

selected_validations.reason: Generated prompts must prevent accidental production drift between repo state and deployed site.
required_evidence: production deploy source is main unless prod is explicitly defined
if prod exists, main/prod sync check is included
generated prompt says not to deploy when origin/main and origin/prod diverge unless explicitly approved
deployed commit SHA must be recorded
Cloudflare Worker name and production domain must be recorded
required GitHub secrets and Cloudflare permissions are listed

selected_validations.reason: The generated launch pack must clearly tell Ferosh what is ready, what is missing, and what to paste/run in the new client repo.
required_evidence: client questions are grouped and actionable
content-needed checklist separates launch-blocking from post-launch items
codex-build.prompt.md can be run in a brand-new repo without requiring private central MDE files
technical assumptions are explicit

selected_validations.reason: The generated client website prompt must require visible, usable conversion paths and avoid fake or misleading actions.
required_evidence: phone/contact/estimate CTA requirements are visible on desktop and mobile
contact form success/failure behavior is specified
click-to-call tel links are required when a phone number exists
navigation includes clear service, service-area, and contact paths
links and buttons must perform useful actions

selected_validations.reason: Generated client websites must feel specific, credible, and human-written; visitors must not see AI, SEO, template, implementation, or internal planning language.
required_evidence: generated build prompt embeds the README public website copy requirements
public routes are scanned for forbidden internal/process phrases before handoff
remaining AI/process wording count is 0
remaining public placeholder pages count is 0
placeholder/sample pages are not public, not linked in navigation, or return 404
service pages describe actual services rather than why a page exists
customer-facing headings replace internal headings
images are real supplied or approved assets with factual captions

selected_validations.reason: Generated public pages must use only verified facts, client-approved content, supplied photos, and approved claims.
required_evidence: no invented testimonials, review snippets, customer names, ratings, review counts, certifications, staff bios, project stories, before/after outcomes, case studies, awards, guarantees, services, locations, or credentials
client-dependent sections are removed from public routes until supplied and approved
client-dependent remaining items are listed only in private handoff or content-needed artifacts

selected_validations.reason: The generator must keep future client repos safe while still carrying enough MDE structure for validation and learning.
required_evidence: MISSION.md seed is generated or described
.mde/project.json seed is generated or described
.mde/validation-strategy.json seed is generated or described
.mde/outbox/events.jsonl guidance is generated or described
docs/client-intake-needed.md is generated or described
docs/content-needed-from-client.md is generated or described
docs/website-build-brief.md is generated or described
docs/launch-validation-plan.md is generated or described
docs/deployment.md is generated or described
central private ledgers, cross-client lessons, and confidential internal strategy are excluded

external_validators.name: generated-output-link-check
external_validators.reason: Useful after sample launch packs include outbound links, but not required during initial Generation 0.

external_validators.name: json-schema-validation
external_validators.reason: Structured generator outputs should remain parseable and suitable for automation.

external_validators.name: public-route-forbidden-phrase-scan
external_validators.reason: Generated client website repos must add a test or script that checks every public customer route for internal/process phrases before handoff and after production deploy.

mde_native_validators: Northvalley repo and Cloudflare deployment checklist

validation_gates.description: Meaningful user-facing or mission-critical work uses a Primary Validation Gate plus a Third-Person Validation Gate. Generation 0 still requires project-specific validation before phase exit.

primary_validation_gate.purpose: Proves the implementation or artifact works functionally and technically.

third_person_validation_gate.purpose: Independently reviews the result from the user's point of view for usability, mission fit, role fit, workflow fit, edge cases, data shape, environment, and integration risks.

third_person_validation_gate.description: The Third-Person Validation Gate is a distinct gate, not a duplicate rerun.

sample_fixtures_required.purpose: Prove the generator creates service-area page requirements without making unsupported claims.
Prove the generator includes AEO and FAQ requirements.
Prove the generator asks for Google Business/Profile evidence and local NAP/service-area content.
Prove the generator requires exact evidence URLs or a verification step.
Prove the generator includes the main/prod sync check before deployment.
Prove the generator requires public route validation that catches AI/process wording, placeholder pages, internal headings, and invented proof.

expensive_validations.validation: Full generated website build and deploy
expensive_validations.reason: Not required for Generator Generation 0; run when validating an actual client website repo generated from the launch pack.
expensive_validations.revisit_when: First end-to-end sample client website build is created.

expensive_validations.reason: Requires real repo, domain, Cloudflare credentials, and client approval.
expensive_validations.revisit_when: A real client website repo is ready for launch validation.

second_run_policy.description: Generation 0 phase exit requires clean project-specific validation, with a Primary Validation Gate and distinct Third-Person Validation Gate for meaningful user-facing or mission-critical work. Risk-based reruns still apply to changed, failed, or high-risk checks.
may_skip_with_reason: external validators marked optional and not relevant to the current fixture
